title: RnS v2: detect silent limit-stops and probe-resume without a recorded reset time
description: Operator incident (hcom #15260, named Rise & Shine/RnS): claude-lab-rose hit its usage limit overnight with no final turn, so it never wrote out_of_tokens/resume_after, and hcom kept listing the stopped session, so neither watcher path fired. v2 fixes both blind spots: (1) classify liveness from hcom list --json status + status_age_seconds instead of bare name presence; (2) for presumed-down agents with no recorded reset time, extract the reset time from the session transcript tail when possible, else probe-resume on a capped backoff schedule covering the 5h window.
acceptance_criteria: Liveness classification uses status + staleness, not list presence; regression test reproduces the overnight incident shape
Presumed-down agents get probe-resumes on a capped backoff (no infinite spawn attempts), cleared when the agent rises
Transcript reset-time extraction used when available, with backoff as fallback, never blocking on it
Protocol note updated and branded RnS; suite passes
objective: Make Rise & Shine actually fire in the real failure mode: sessions that die with no final turn and no status record, while still visible in hcom listings.
required_context: MAP_System/agents/limit-watcher-state.json (overnight evidence: last_live never changed, silent_reported empty)
hcom list --json fields: status, status_age_seconds, transcript_path
forbidden_changes: No unbounded probe loops -- hard cap on attempts per incident
No blocking/slow transcript parsing in the poll loop (tail-read only, bounded bytes)
Do not remove v1 behavior (recorded resume_after path stays primary)
expected_artifacts: limit_watcher.py v2 with liveness classification + probe-resume + transcript reset extraction
tests reproducing the overnight incident shape
protocol note updated (RnS branding + v2 behavior)
risk: Medium: probe-resumes spawn paid sessions on a schedule without a known reset time. Mitigations: grace period before first probe, capped backoff (6 attempts/window), one incident record per down-event, give-up BLOCKED event, dry-run mode.
